pattern: Pass the parent and grandparent values down during DFS; when the grandparent value is even, add the current node's value to a running total.
hints: A node's grandparent is two levels above it — thread two extra values (parent-val, grandparent-val) through the recursive call rather than navigating up.
At each node call left and right children with the current node's value as the new parent, and the incoming parent value as the new grandparent.
If the grandparent value passed in is even (and nonzero, i.e. a real grandparent exists), accumulate the current node's value.

edge_inputs.description: single node — no grandparent, sum is 0
two-level tree — no node has a grandparent
three-level tree with even root grandparent
odd grandparent does not contribute even though parent is even
all node values equal 1 (all odd grandparents)
multiple even-valued grandparents at different levels

stress.description: balanced binary tree of depth ~7 with random node values 1-100
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nodes = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    # build BFS-level tree representation
    result = []
    for v in nodes:
        result.append(v)
    return [result]
